cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Market Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI has hired a top executive from Uber India to lead its operations in the region. The move signals aggressive expansion of offices, partnerships, and hiring in India, one of OpenAI's biggest markets outside the U.S.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom to reduce reliance on Nvidia. The move joins Google, Apple, and SpaceX in the growing trend of major tech companies developing their own AI chips.

cards.title: AI Model Competition Transcends Company Rivalry
cards.body: AI capabilities have progressed to the point where they carry real political consequences. Addressing these consequences requires collective action beyond traditional company-to-company competition.

cards.title: White House Asks OpenAI to Slow Model Release
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI delay the public release of GPT 5.6 due to safety concerns. OpenAI plans to share the model with select partners instead of broader public release.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for Agent Testing
cards.body: Patronus AI, founded by former Meta researchers, secured $50 million to build digital stress-testing environments for AI agents. The startup is experiencing strong demand as AI agent development accelerates.

cards.title: Claude Gaining Ground on Paid ChatGPT Users
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead. Data shows a notable shift in consumer preference toward Claude among paying users.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game-Based AI
cards.body: General Intuition secured $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage. The company believes gaming data can help develop AI with more human-like intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Chief Eyes 1000x Power Reduction
cards.body: Un-0, an image generation tool from Databricks' former AI chief, demonstrates technology that could slash AI power consumption dramatically. The system replicates conventional AI results with significantly lower energy requirements.

cards.title: Netris Secures $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: Netris raised $15 million to accelerate neocloud deployment with network switch software. The platform helps operators reduce time-to-live for AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for Enhancement Tools
cards.body: Adobe completed its acquisition of image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs. Adobe plans to integrate Topaz tools across its creative applications.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13 billion investment in AI infrastructure across India. The move reflects intense competition among global tech giants expanding AI capabilities in the region.

cards.title: Europe Challenges U.S. Chip Export Restrictions
cards.body: European officials are pushing back against Washington's expanding chip trade controls affecting allies. The MATCH Act's restrictions on older chipmaking equipment are creating friction with traditional partners.

cards.title: Infosys Alum Launches IT Services Challenger
cards.body: Former Infosys Chief Vishal Sikka launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The venture combines talent from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to disrupt IT services.

cards.title: Cerebras Stock Plunges After Earnings Report
cards.body: Cerebras stock fell sharply following its first public earnings report with narrower gross margin forecasts. The AI chipmaker's CEO clarified that profit margin outlook had been misunderstood.

cards.title: Engineering Jobs Prove Resilient Against AI
cards.body: New data shows engineering roles are among the most resilient job categories despite AI disruption. Engineers actually represent a larger share of new hires than many expected.

cards.title: Top AI Researchers Exit Google for Anthropic
cards.body: Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are among the latest Google AI researchers departing for Anthropic. The departures continue a trend of top talent leaving Google for competitive AI labs.

cards.title: Memory Chip Shortage Boosts U.S. Company Revenue
cards.body: A major U.S. memory chip manufacturer saw revenue quadruple to $41.45 billion year-over-year. Profits surged from $1.88 billion to $28.2 billion, driven by persistent chip demand.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Market Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI is expanding its presence in India with a major executive hire from Uber. The move signals the company's commitment to growing offices, partnerships, and hiring in its biggest market outside the U.S.

cards.title: Tech Giants Building Custom AI Chips to Challenge Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary AI chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia. OpenAI's Jalapeño inference chip, built with Broadcom, signals a major shift in the semiconductor landscape.

cards.title: White House Asks OpenAI to Slow Model Release
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI delay releasing GPT 5.6 to a broader public due to safety concerns. The model will instead be shared with a select group of partners.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI secured $50 million in funding led by former Meta AI researchers. The company creates digital worlds to stress-test AI agents amid surging demand.

cards.title: Claude Gaining Ground in Paid AI Market
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead. Data shows a growing shift toward Claude among subscribed AI users.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game-Trained AI
cards.body: The startup raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage. The approach aims to help AI agents develop human-like intuition through gameplay data.

cards.title: Ex-Databricks AI Chief Aims to Cut Power Usage
cards.body: Un-0, a new image-generation system, demonstrates technology that could slash AI's power consumption by 1,000x. The breakthrough challenges conventional assumptions about AI computational costs.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A for AI Infrastructure
cards.body: a16z-backed Netris provides software for network switches that accelerates neocloud deployment. The platform helps operators reduce time-to-live for AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for AI Enhancement Tools
cards.body: Adobe announced the acquisition of image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs. The tools will be integrated across Adobe's creative applications.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B More in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13 billion investment in AI infrastructure for India. The move reflects the global tech industry's race to expand AI capabilities in the region.

cards.title: AI Was Supposed to Kill Jobs; Engineers Thrive
cards.body: New data from SignalFire shows engineers are actually hiring at higher rates despite AI disruption narratives. Engineers represent a growing share of new hires across the tech industry.

cards.title: Top AI Researchers Leaving Google for Anthropic
cards.body: Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are departing Google to join Anthropic, following other notable scientist exits. The trend reflects ongoing talent migration to AI competitors.

cards.title: AI Reshapes Retail Behind-the-Scenes Operations
cards.body: Artificial intelligence is transforming retail decision-making in inventory management and search results. The changes occur mostly invisibly, not through flashy consumer-facing features.

cards.title: IBM Chip Technology Could Extend Moore's Law
cards.body: IBM unveiled a prototype chip with 100 billion transistors at twice previous density levels. The breakthrough could enable faster, more energy-efficient computers for the next decade.

cards.title: Stripe, Anthropic, OpenAI Back Infection Research
cards.body: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are funding efforts to prevent respiratory infections like the common cold. The collaboration aims to develop new prevention strategies for widespread viral infections.

cards.title: Web Data Infrastructure Layer Emerging for AI
cards.body: Enterprises need massive data at scale to capitalize on AI potential, but much relevant information remains blocked or unstructured. A new infrastructure layer is addressing data accessibility challenges for AI models.

cards.title: Notion Shutters Email App as Users Prefer AI
cards.body: Notion is discontinuing its Skiff-influenced email app because most users now prefer AI agent alternatives. The shift reflects changing user preferences toward AI-powered productivity tools.

cards.title: Anthropic Claims Alibaba Cloned Claude Model
cards.body: Anthropic accused Alibaba of the largest Claude cloning attack to date. The company is calling for government punishment against the alleged unauthorized model replication.

cards.title: IBM Claims World's First Sub-1 Nanometer Tech
cards.body: IBM announced breakthrough chip technology achieving sub-1 nanometer specifications. The advancement could represent a major leap forward in semiconductor performance and efficiency.

cards.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Announce LLM Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI and Broadcom jointly unveiled a custom chip designed for large-scale LLM inference. The collaboration represents a significant step toward specialized AI hardware infrastructure.
